vulnerability.title: Prevent Login to Accounts With Empty Password
vulnerability.description: If an account is configured for password authentication but does not have an assigned password, it may be possible to log into the account without authentication. Remove any instances of the nullok in /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth to prevent logins with empty passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if [ -f /usr/bin/authselect ]; then if ! authselect check; then echo " authselect integrity check failed. Remediation aborted! This remediation could not be applied because an authselect profile was not selected or the selected profile is not intact. It is not recommended to manually edit the PAM files when authselect tool is available. In cases where the default authselect profile does not cover a specific demand, a custom authselect profile is recommended." exit 1 fi authselect enable-feature without-nullok authselect apply-changes -b else if grep -qP "^\s*auth\s+sufficient\s+pam_unix.so\s.*\bnullok\b" "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"; then sed -i -E --follow-symlinks "s/(.*auth.*sufficient.*pam_unix.so.*)\

triage_decision.reason: Removing the nullok option from pam_unix entries eliminates the ability to authenticate with an empty password, which aligns with the STIG requirement and does not inherently affect system boot or core services. The change is limited to PAM configuration and can be applied using authselect, minimizing the risk of lockout when performed correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential lockout of accounts that currently rely on empty passwords; Misapplication of the change if authselect profile is not intact
